data.reasoningText:
Now I can see the issue clearly. This is a tiny project (single file, ~45 lines of code) that has an extreme number of Roslyn analyzers stacked on it:

From the `.csproj`:
1. `Roslynator.Analyzers` 4.7.0
2. `SonarAnalyzer.CSharp` 9.16.0.82469
3. `Meziantou.Analyzer` 2.0.127

From `Directory.Build.props`:
4. `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers` 8.0.0 (GlobalPackageReference)


5. `StyleCop.Analyzers` 1.1.118 (GlobalPackageReference)

Plus the project has code style enforcement enabled during build, analysis level set to latest-recommended, documentation generation turned on, and warnings treated as errors. That's five different analyzer packages running on a single-file project with strict settings, so the analyzers are almost certainly the bottleneck here. I'm waiting for the build-perf agent to come back with actual timing data to confirm.

# 🔍 Build Performance Diagnosis: Contoso.WebApi

## Project Profile
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Source files** | 1 file (DataService.cs — 45 lines) |
| **Project type** | .NET 8.0 class library |
| **Analyzer DLLs loaded** | **27** |
| **Package references** | 4 in .csproj + 2 GlobalPackageReference in Directory.Build.props |

## Measured Build Times

| Scenario | Duration | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Full build (with analyzers)** | **4.08s** | Successful build with `/p:TreatWarningsAsErrors=false` |
| **Build (no analyzers)** | **0.60s** | With `/p:RunAnalyzers=false --no-restore` |
| **No-op rebuild (with analyzers)** | **0.80s** | Nothing changed, analyzers still re-run |

> **Analyzers account for ~85% of total build time** — 3.48s out of 4.08s is pure analyzer overhead on a 45-line file.

## Task Performance Summary (from binlog)

| Task | Time | % of Build |
|---|---|---|
| `RestoreTask` | 914ms | 22% |
| `Csc` (compilation + analyzers) | 468ms | 11% |
| `MSBuild` (orchestration) | 286ms | 7% |
| `ProcessFrameworkReferences` | 111ms | 3% |
| `ResolvePackageAssets` | 103ms | 3% |
| `ResolveAssemblyReference` | 60ms | 1% |
| Everything else | ~2,138ms | 53% (mostly analyzer loading/init) |

## Root Causes Identified

### 🔴 Issue #1: Extreme Analyzer Overload (PRIMARY — ~85% of build time)

**Five** separate analyzer packages are loaded — producing **27 analyzer DLLs** for the compiler to load, initialize, and run against 45 lines of code:

| Package | Source | DLLs Loaded | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Roslynator.Analyzers** 4.7.0 | .csproj | 11 | Huge analyzer suite |
| **SonarAnalyzer.CSharp** 9.16.0 | .csproj | 4 | Includes Google.Protobuf.dll |
| **Meziantou.Analyzer** 2.0.127 | .csproj | 2 | |
| **Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers** 8.0.0 | Directory.Build.props | 2 | ⚠️ **REDUNDANT** — see Issue #2 |
| **StyleCop.Analyzers** 1.1.118 | Directory.Build.props | (failed to load) | Legacy version, may not be compatible |
| SDK built-in analyzers | SDK | 6 | Runtime source generators |
| **CodeStyle analyzers** (from `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`) | SDK | 4 | See Issue #3 |

### 🔴 Issue #2: Duplicate NetAnalyzers

In `Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<GlobalPackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" Version="8.0.0" />
```
This is **completely redundant** because `<AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>` (also in Directory.Build.props) already causes the SDK to include its own built-in copy of these exact same analyzers. The SDK version (from .NET 11 SDK in this environment) is actually **newer** than the 8.0.0 NuGet package, so the NuGet package is both redundant and stale.

### 🟡 Issue #3: `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` Adds 4 More Analyzer DLLs

```xml
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
```
This loads 4 additional CodeStyle analyzer DLLs (`Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.CodeStyle.dll`, etc.) to enforce IDE-prefixed rules (IDE0008, IDE0011, IDE0028, IDE0160, IDE0290) during build. These are normally IDE-only rules — enforcing them in build is expensive and overlaps with Roslynator/StyleCop which cover many of the same style concerns.

### 🟡 Issue #4: Massive Rule Overlap Between Analyzers

Multiple packages flag the same issues, wasting time:
- **Globalization**: `CA1305` (NetAnalyzers) **AND** `MA0011` (Meziantou) — both flagged line 41
- **String comparison**: `CA1307`-style (NetAnalyzers) **AND** `MA0002` (Meziantou) — both flagged
- **Code style**: IDE rules (CodeStyle) **AND** Roslynator **AND** StyleCop — triple coverage
- **Logging**: `CA1848`/`CA1873` (NetAnalyzers) overlap with Meziantou logging rules

### 🟡 Issue #5: `GenerateDocumentationFile` + `TreatWarningsAsErrors` = Build Failure

```xml
<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
```
This combination causes the default build to **fail** (22 errors, all from analyzers/CS1591). No code change can be compiled without first writing XML docs for every public member and satisfying all 5 analyzer suites simultaneously.

### 🟡 Issue #6: StyleCop.Analyzers 1.1.118 is Outdated

Version 1.1.118 is the legacy non-source-generator version. If StyleCop is needed, version `1.2.0-beta.*` uses source generators and is significantly faster.

---

## Recommendations (Prioritized)

### 🏆 Quick Win #1: Remove Redundant `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers` GlobalPackageReference

**Impact: Eliminates duplicate analyzer loading. Saves ~0.3–0.5s.**

In `Directory.Build.props`, remove:
```xml
<!-- DELETE THIS LINE — already included by the SDK via AnalysisLevel -->
<GlobalPackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" Version="8.0.0" />
```
The SDK's `<AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>` already activates these analyzers at a newer version.

### 🏆 Quick Win #2: Consolidate to ONE Analyzer Suite

**Impact: Could reduce Csc time by 60–70%. Estimated savings: 2–3s.**

You have **5 overlapping analyzer packages**. Pick ONE primary suite and remove the rest:

**Option A — Keep only Meziantou (recommended for this project size):**
```xml
<!-- Contoso.WebApi.csproj — AFTER cleanup -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.2" />
  <PackageReference Include="Meziantou.Analyzer" Version="2.0.127" />
</ItemGroup>
```
Remove: `Roslynator.Analyzers`, `SonarAnalyzer.CSharp`, `StyleCop.Analyzers` GlobalPackageReference.

**Option B — Keep Roslynator + SDK analyzers only** (if you prefer Roslynator's rule set).

SonarAnalyzer in particular is very expensive (loads Google.Protobuf.dll, a control flow graph analysis library, etc.) and is best run in a **dedicated CI step via SonarQube/SonarCloud** rather than on every local build.

### 🏆 Quick Win #3: Disable `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` or Move to CI-only

**Impact: Removes 4 CodeStyle analyzer DLLs. Saves ~0.3–0.5s.**

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>false</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
```
Or keep it CI-only:
```xml
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild Condition="'$(CI)' == 'true'">true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
```

### 🥈 Medium Effort #4: Run Heavy Analyzers Only in CI

**Impact: Developers get fast local builds; CI still enforces all rules.**

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(CI)' != 'true'">
  <RunAnalyzers>false</RunAnalyzers>
  <!-- Or selectively: -->
  <!-- <RunAnalyzersDuringBuild>false</RunAnalyzersDuringBuild> -->
</PropertyGroup>
```
Developers rely on IDE real-time analysis; the CI pipeline runs the full analyzer suite.

### 🥈 Medium Effort #5: Suppress CS1591 or Remove `GenerateDocumentationFile`

**Impact: Fixes the build failure; reduces noise.**

If XML docs aren't consumed by anything:
```xml
<GenerateDocumentationFile>false</GenerateDocumentationFile>
```
If XML docs are needed (e.g., for Swagger/NuGet):
```xml
<NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591</NoWarn>
```

### 🥈 Medium Effort #6: Upgrade or Remove StyleCop.Analyzers

**Impact: Avoids loading a legacy analyzer.**

Either remove entirely (since Roslynator/Meziantou/CodeStyle cover the same ground) or upgrade:
```xml
<!-- If keeping: upgrade to source-generator version -->
<GlobalPackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" Version="1.2.0-beta.556" />
```

---

## Projected Impact

| Scenario | Current | After Recommendations | Improvement |
|---|---|---|---|
| Full build (cold) | 4.08s | ~1.0–1.5s | **60–75% faster** |
| No-op rebuild | 0.80s | ~0.3–0.4s | **50–60% faster** |
| Analyzer DLLs loaded | 27 | ~8–10 | **63–70% fewer** |

## Summary

The root cause is clear: **this 45-line project loads 27 analyzer DLLs from 5 overlapping packages**. The analyzer initialization and execution dominates 85% of build time. The top three actions — removing the redundant NetAnalyzers package, consolidating to one analyzer suite, and disabling `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` — would reduce build time by approximately 60–75% with minimal risk.

The Csc task itself only takes 468 milliseconds when the analyzers are loaded and executing.

Breaking down the build targets by duration, Restore takes the longest at 914 milliseconds, followed by CoreCompile at 484 milliseconds, then the restore graph initialization at 200 milliseconds.

The core issue is that five separate analyzer packages are installed—Roslynator with 11 DLLs, SonarAnalyzer with 4 DLLs, Meziantou with 2 DLLs, and Microsoft's NetAnalyzers with 2 DLLs—and they're all being loaded and run against this tiny file, creating massive overhead.

There's also redundancy: NetAnalyzers is referenced globally in Directory.Build.props even though the SDK already includes it when `AnalysisLevel=latest-recommended` is set, so we're loading the same analyzers twice. On top of that, enabling `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true` pulls in additional IDE code style analyzers that add even more processing time.

Then there's `GenerateDocumentationFile=true` combined with `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`, which forces the compiler to generate documentation and triggers CS1591 errors for every undocumented public member. And across all these packages, there's significant overlap — NetAnalyzers, Meziantou, Roslynator, and StyleCop are all checking similar concerns like globalization, logging, and code style, so we're essentially running the same checks multiple times.

| **No-op rebuild (with analyzers)** | **0.80s** | Nothing changed, analyzers still re-run |

> **Analyzers account for ~85% of total build time** — 3.48s out of 4.08s is pure analyzer overhead on a 45-line file.

## Task Performance Summary (from binlog)

| Task | Time | % of Build |
|---|---|---|
| `RestoreTask` | 914ms | 22% |
| `Csc` (compilation + analyzers) | 468ms | 11% |
| `MSBuild` (orchestration) | 286ms | 7% |
| `ProcessFrameworkReferences` | 111ms | 3% |
| `ResolvePackageAssets` | 103ms | 3% |
| `ResolveAssemblyReference` | 60ms | 1% |
| Everything else | ~2,138ms | 53% (mostly analyzer loading/init) |

## Root Causes Identified

### 🔴 Issue #1: Extreme Analyzer Overload (PRIMARY — ~85% of build time)

**Five** separate analyzer packages are loaded — producing **27 analyzer DLLs** for the compiler to load, initialize, and run against 45 lines of code:

| Package | Source | DLLs Loaded | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Roslynator.Analyzers** 4.7.0 | .csproj | 11 | Huge analyzer suite |
| **SonarAnalyzer.CSharp** 9.16.0 | .csproj | 4 | Includes Google.Protobuf.dll |
| **Meziantou.Analyzer** 2.0.127 | .csproj | 2 | |
| **Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers** 8.0.0 | Directory.Build.props | 2 | ⚠️ **REDUNDANT** — see Issue #2 |
| **StyleCop.Analyzers** 1.1.118 | Directory.Build.props | (failed to load) | Legacy version, may not be compatible |
| SDK built-in analyzers | SDK | 6 | Runtime source generators |
| **CodeStyle analyzers** (from `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`) | SDK | 4 | See Issue #3 |

### 🔴 Issue #2: Duplicate NetAnalyzers

In `Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<GlobalPackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" Version="8.0.0" />
```
This is **completely redundant** because `<AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>` (also in Directory.Build.props) already causes the SDK to include its own built-in copy of these exact same analyzers. The SDK version (from .NET 11 SDK in this environment) is actually **newer** than the 8.0.0 NuGet package, so the NuGet package is both redundant and stale.

### 🟡 Issue #3: `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` Adds 4 More Analyzer DLLs

```xml
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
```
This loads 4 additional CodeStyle analyzer DLLs (`Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.CodeStyle.dll`, etc.) to enforce IDE-prefixed rules (IDE0008, IDE0011, IDE0028, IDE0160, IDE0290) during build. These are normally IDE-only rules — enforcing them in build is expensive and overlaps with Roslynator/StyleCop which cover many of the same style concerns.

### 🟡 Issue #4: Massive Rule Overlap Between Analyzers

Multiple packages flag the same issues, wasting time:
- **Globalization**: `CA1305` (NetAnalyzers) **AND** `MA0011` (Meziantou) — both flagged line 41
- **String comparison**: `CA1307`-style (NetAnalyzers) **AND** `MA0002` (Meziantou) — both flagged
- **Code style**: IDE rules (CodeStyle) **AND** Roslynator **AND** StyleCop — triple coverage
- **Logging**: `CA1848`/`CA1873` (NetAnalyzers) overlap with Meziantou logging rules

### 🟡 Issue #5: `GenerateDocumentationFile` + `TreatWarningsAsErrors` = Build Failure

```xml
<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
```
This combination causes the default build to **fail** (22 errors, all from analyzers/CS1591). No code change can be compiled without first writing XML docs for every public member and satisfying all 5 analyzer suites simultaneously.

### 🟡 Issue #6: StyleCop.Analyzers 1.1.118 is Outdated

Version 1.1.118 is the legacy non-source-generator version. If StyleCop is needed, version `1.2.0-beta.*` uses source generators and is significantly faster.

---

## Recommendations (Prioritized)

### 🏆 Quick Win #1: Remove Redundant `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers` GlobalPackageReference

**Impact: Eliminates duplicate analyzer loading. Saves ~0.3–0.5s.**

In `Directory.Build.props`, remove:
```xml
<!-- DELETE THIS LINE — already included by the SDK via AnalysisLevel -->
<GlobalPackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" Version="8.0.0" />
```
The SDK's `<AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>` already activates these analyzers at a newer version.

### 🏆 Quick Win #2: Consolidate to ONE Analyzer Suite

**Impact: Could reduce Csc time by 60–70%. Estimated savings: 2–3s.**

You have **5 overlapping analyzer packages**. Pick ONE primary suite and remove the rest:

**Option A — Keep only Meziantou (recommended for this project size):**
```xml
<!-- Contoso.WebApi.csproj — AFTER cleanup -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.2" />
  <PackageReference Include="Meziantou.Analyzer" Version="2.0.127" />
</ItemGroup>
```
Remove: `Roslynator.Analyzers`, `SonarAnalyzer.CSharp`, `StyleCop.Analyzers` GlobalPackageReference.

**Option B — Keep Roslynator + SDK analyzers only** (if you prefer Roslynator's rule set).

SonarAnalyzer in particular is very expensive (loads Google.Protobuf.dll, a control flow graph analysis library, etc.) and is best run in a **dedicated CI step via SonarQube/SonarCloud** rather than on every local build.

### 🏆 Quick Win #3: Disable `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` or Move to CI-only

**Impact: Removes 4 CodeStyle analyzer DLLs. Saves ~0.3–0.5s.**

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>false</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
```
Or keep it CI-only:
```xml
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild Condition="'$(CI)' == 'true'">true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
```

### 🥈 Medium Effort #4: Run Heavy Analyzers Only in CI

**Impact: Developers get fast local builds; CI still enforces all rules.**

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(CI)' != 'true'">
  <RunAnalyzers>false</RunAnalyzers>
  <!-- Or selectively: -->
  <!-- <RunAnalyzersDuringBuild>false</RunAnalyzersDuringBuild> -->
</PropertyGroup>
```
Developers rely on IDE real-time analysis; the CI pipeline runs the full analyzer suite.

### 🥈 Medium Effort #5: Suppress CS1591 or Remove `GenerateDocumentationFile`

**Impact: Fixes the build failure; reduces noise.**

If XML docs aren't consumed by anything:
```xml
<GenerateDocumentationFile>false</GenerateDocumentationFile>
```
If XML docs are needed (e.g., for Swagger/NuGet):
```xml
<NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591</NoWarn>
```

### 🥈 Medium Effort #6: Upgrade or Remove StyleCop.Analyzers

**Impact: Avoids loading a legacy analyzer.**

Either remove entirely (since Roslynator/Meziantou/CodeStyle cover the same ground) or upgrade:
```xml
<!-- If keeping: upgrade to source-generator version -->
<GlobalPackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" Version="1.2.0-beta.556" />
```

---

## Projected Impact

| Scenario | Current | After Recommendations | Improvement |
|---|---|---|---|
| Full build (cold) | 4.08s | ~1.0–1.5s | **60–75% faster** |
| No-op rebuild | 0.80s | ~0.3–0.4s | **50–60% faster** |
| Analyzer DLLs loaded | 27 | ~8–10 | **63–70% fewer** |

## Summary

The root cause is clear: **this 45-line project loads 27 analyzer DLLs from 5 overlapping packages**. The analyzer initialization and execution dominates 85% of build time. The top three actions — removing the redundant NetAnalyzers package, consolidating to one analyzer suite, and disabling `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` — would reduce build time by approximately 60–75% with minimal risk.
